notes: IBM 64-bit POWER server line (RS/6000 1990, AIX platform) and derived PowerPC (1991 Apple/IBM/Motorola AIM alliance); opened via Power.org 2004; still active in IBM i / AIX / Linux-on-Power
Intel 8086-derived ISA; 16-bit (1978), 32-bit (1985), 64-bit via AMD64 (2003) and Intel 64 (2004); dominant desktop/server ISA
Intel StrongARM successor; ARMv5TE architecture; PXA PDA/smartphone chips; sold to Marvell Jun 2006 for $600M; exited ARM business
Low-cost application-specific microprocessors for consumer electronics, automotive, industrial; ARM, MIPS, PowerPC, RISC-V dominate; Intel x86 largely absent
32/64-bit RISC CPU architecture; dominant mobile/embedded ISA; basis of Apple Silicon, Qualcomm, MediaTek, NVIDIA Grace; acquired by SoftBank 2016, Nasdaq IPO 2023
